title: Uses of Grape Extracts and Phytochemicals as Antiaging Agents. The aging process is closely linked to oxidative stress and the breakdown of skin structural proteins, and polyphenolic compounds from fresh grapes, grape marcs, and wine lees, especially resveratrol, exhibit significant antioxidant activity and inhibitory effects on enzymes linked to skin aging. Recent studies investigating the extracts from grape seeds and skins have reported multifaceted antiaging effects, including telomere lengthening, increased telomerase activity, and regulation of the activity of enzymes involved in extracellular matrix remodeling, such as elastase, matrix metalloproteinase-1, and tyrosinase. In one study, researchers studied 59 grape seed flavonoids and found that 14 of them displayed activity in preventing the demethylation of protein phosphatase 2A, an enzyme involved in regulating oxidative stress in skin cells. Quercetagetin, myricetin, and sciadopitysin showed the highest P P 2 A activation activity among all tested flavonoids. By enhancing P P 2 A methylation, grape seed extracts may help combat aging processes and improve skin health. Gallotannin from grapes has also been shown to have antiaging effects mediated via inhibiting the activity of elastolytic enzymes and reducing ultraviolet B-induced damage to human skin cells. In vitro studies have shown that gallotannin can improve collagen synthesis, reduce the expression of matrix metalloproteinase-1, and modulate the E R K and J N K signaling pathways, which are involved in cellular stress and apoptosis.

sentences.text: Uses of Grape Extracts and Phytochemicals as Antiaging Agents.

The aging process is closely linked to oxidative stress and the breakdown of skin structural proteins, and polyphenolic compounds from fresh grapes, grape marcs, and wine lees, especially resveratrol, exhibit significant antioxidant activity and inhibitory effects on enzymes linked to skin aging.

Recent studies investigating the extracts from grape seeds and skins have reported multifaceted antiaging effects, including telomere lengthening, increased telomerase activity, and regulation of the activity of enzymes involved in extracellular matrix remodeling, such as elastase, matrix metalloproteinase-1, and tyrosinase.

In one study, researchers studied 59 grape seed flavonoids and found that 14 of them displayed activity in preventing the demethylation of protein phosphatase 2A, an enzyme involved in regulating oxidative stress in skin cells.
Quercetagetin, myricetin, and sciadopitysin showed the highest P P 2 A activation activity among all tested flavonoids.
By enhancing P P 2 A methylation, grape seed extracts may help combat aging processes and improve skin health.
Gallotannin from grapes has also been shown to have antiaging effects mediated via inhibiting the activity of elastolytic enzymes and reducing ultraviolet B-induced damage to human skin cells.

In vitro studies have shown that gallotannin can improve collagen synthesis, reduce the expression of matrix metalloproteinase-1, and modulate the E R K and J N K signaling pathways, which are involved in cellular stress and apoptosis.
Recent advancements in nanoparticle technology have enhanced the delivery and stability of grape-derived polyphenols.

For example, nanoparticles encapsulating grape seed oil, coenzyme Q10, and retinyl palmitate have shown promising antiaging effects, with significant reductions in the appearance of periorbital wrinkles and improvement in skin hydration.

Nanoemulsion formulations of grape pomace extracts and grape seed extracts encapsulated in soy lecithin microdispersions have been found biocompatible with keratinocytes, protecting the cells from oxidative damage and exhibiting excellent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

A novel grape seed oil-loaded dermocosmetic nanoemulgel formulation was tested in a placebo-controlled study to assess its effects on the facial skin of 15 healthy volunteers over a period of 12 weeks.
Parameters such as skin melanin, erythema, sebum production, pore size, elasticity, and moisture levels were measured using a variety of instruments.
The results demonstrated that the nanoemulgel formulation significantly improved skin moisture and elasticity while reducing melanin, erythema, and pore size compared to the placebo.

Sensory scores regarding appearance, odor, consistency, and spreadability of the formulation were also significantly higher for the nanoemulgel, highlighting its potential as an effective skin care agent.
Grape seed oil is rich in tocopherols and unsaturated fatty acids, such as linoleic, palmitic, stearic, and oleic acids.
These substances are categorized as emollients due to their ability to moisturize, soften, and smooth the skin while also providing a protective barrier.
Clinical investigations have also shown the antiaging property of resveratrol.

In a 12-week clinical study including 55 women between the ages of 40 and 60, it was found that night cream containing 1 percent resveratrol, 0.5 percent baicalin, and 1 percent vitamin E could improve skin health with significant increase in skin elasticity and firmness while reducing fine wrinkles and skin pigmentation.
This was supported by a placebo-controlled, double-blind study in 50 subjects who received a dietary supplement containing resveratrol and procyanidin for 60 days.
This treatment improved skin health and reduced roughness and wrinkles.

Likewise, topical application of a formulation containing resveratrol, green tea polyphenols, and caffeine led to skin rejuvenation and reduced facial redness in 16 subjects during 8 to 12 weeks of daily application.

The beneficial effects of these formulations are due to multiple bioactivities, such as the effect on gene expression of hem oxygenase-1, vascular endothelial growth factor A, and collagen III alpha 1, among other mechanisms.

Hemoxygenase-1 protects skin cells against the cytotoxic effects of reactive oxygen species by activating endogenous antioxidant enzymes, whereas V E G F A reduces vascular permeability and inflammation in the skin, thereby decreasing facial redness.
Enhanced collagen synthesis further improves skin elasticity and reduces wrinkles.

In summary, grape products have gained recognition in the cosmetic industry due to their potential in combating signs of aging, providing photoprotection, maintaining skin hydration and elasticity, and improving overall skin health.
